content.text: Real DOM + CSS · shared with your agent
Design the interface. Ship the same thing.
Pitolet is a browser-based design canvas that you and your coding agent can edit together. It uses real web layout, so the design already behaves like the page you will ship.
A calmer way to plan the week.
Projects, decisions, and next steps in one clear workspace.
Use real flexbox, grid, breakpoints, and interaction states. Text wraps and layouts respond the way they do on the web.
Connect any MCP client. Your agent can read the open document, follow comments, edit nodes, and update tokens while you watch.
Export React and Tailwind or plain HTML and CSS from the same document. Your tokens, components, and responsive rules come with it.
Start with the site you already have.
Run one local command to capture an existing page at mobile, tablet, and desktop widths. Pitolet keeps the DOM it understands editable and preserves the rest as images.
Mobile styles form the base; wider layouts become breakpoint overrides.
Images move into Pitolet’s asset store instead of staying hotlinked.
Source, imported, and difference images show where the result needs attention.
This page was designed in Pitolet and exported from the same document.
Use the cloud, or run it yourself.
For personal projects and a first workspace.
For teams that need more documents, history, and sharing.
Keep the files and server on your own machine.
